chunk_summary: This section outlines age- and sex-specific energy requirements for Indian children based on ICMR-NIN 2020 values, emphasizing practical application in clinical settings and individualized adjustments for activity, growth, and weight management.
learning_objectives: Understand the ICMR-NIN 2020 energy requirement values for Indian children across different age and sex groups.
Learn how to individualize energy targets based on activity level, growth needs, and weight management.
Apply practical strategies to address common energy intake errors in children and adolescents.
key_facts: Energy requirements for children (moderate activity): 0–6 months: 530 kcal/day; 6–12 months: 660 kcal/day; 1–3 years: 1110 kcal/day; 4–6 years: 1360 kcal/day; 7–9 years: 1700 kcal/day.
Energy requirements for boys: 10–12 years: 2220 kcal/day; 13–15 years: 2860 kcal/day; 16–18 years: 3320 kcal/day.
Energy requirements for girls: 10–12 years: 2060 kcal/day; 13–15 years: 2400 kcal/day; 16–18 years: 2500 kcal/day.
Small children require more energy per kg of body weight compared to adolescents.
Adjust energy targets for high activity, cold exposure, or catch-up growth, and reduce them for excess weight gain or inactivity.
Practical strategies include correcting dilute feeds in infants, addressing skipped meals in adolescents, and reducing sugary beverages across all ages.
reference_quote: The ICMR-NIN 2020 brief note provides age- and sex-specific estimated energy requirements for moderate activity.
document_key_topics: Age- and sex-specific energy requirements for Indian children
Practical application of energy targets in clinical settings
Adjustments for activity, growth, and weight management
